sections.paragraph:
πλημμυρίς (πλημμῠρίς, ίδος, ἡ,)

1. a rise of the sea, πλημμυρὶς ἐκ πόντοιο of the wave caused by the rock thrown by the Cyclops, Od.: flood-tide (cf. ραχία), opp. to ἄμπωτις (ebb), Hdt.

2. generally, a flood, deluge, Arist.; of tears, Aesch., Eur.


Etym. ῠ in Hom., ῡ in attic
Etym. Deriv. uncertain: perh. from πλήθω, μύρω.
